title: Mali Releases 100+ Jihadist Detainees to End JNIM Fuel Blockade
detail: Mali's military junta finalized a controversial deal with JNIM, releasing over 100 suspected jihadist detainees in exchange for the militant group opening a corridor to allow fuel tanker convoys to reach Bamako. The agreement ends a seven-month blockade (September 2025–March 2026) that had caused fuel prices to surge by more than 400 percent, forced school and business closures, and disrupted electricity supply. A local elected official confirmed the prisoner release to AFP. The truce is reportedly valid until Eid al-Adha (late May 2026), after which the situation remains unresolved. Analysts warn the deal sets a dangerous precedent that incentivizes further economic warfare by jihadist groups.

media.caption: Mali releases 100+ jihadist detainees to end JNIM fuel blockade

title: Guinea Dismantles GSIM/JNIM Terrorist Network, Arrests Multiple Suspects
detail: Guinea's General Prosecutor's Office announced the dismantling of a suspected terrorist network linked to GSIM (JNIM), with multiple individuals arrested and charged with terrorism, criminal association, and financing of terrorism. Investigations revealed GSIM-affiliated WhatsApp groups with over 500 members, including 38 Guineans, used to radicalize and coordinate operations. Guinean armed forces had already conducted an operation in the east, near the Malian border, detaining individuals suspected of supporting JNIM logistics and recruitment. Two identified suspects — Ibrahima Sow and Cheick Ibrahima Savane — were charged before a Kaloum district court. Authorities cited the southward expansion of Sahel jihadism as a major threat to Guinea's national security.
